name: Vulture's Balcony Trail
name_original: El Balcón de los Buitres – running circular desde Irurtzun
description: Jog this moderate 3.1-mile circular route from Irurtzun to the Vulture's Balcony for panoramic views and diverse terrain.
region_intro: Jogging routes around Irurtzun traverse a diverse and mountainous landscape within the Foral Community of Navarra, Spain. The region is characterized by steep terrain, including the Sierra de Aralar and the peaks of Andía, providing varied elevations for runners. Features such as Monte Erga, the Larraun River, and the "Dos Hermanas" Gorge contribute to the natural environment. The area also includes sections of the Plazaola Greenway, offering paths along an old railway line. Best jogging routes around Irurtzun The most popular jogging route is Vulture's Balcony Trail, a 3.1 miles (5.0 km) trail that takes about 45 minutes to complete. This moderate route offers panoramic vistas of the Sakana corridor and the Irurtzun valley. Another top favourite among local runners is Erga loop from Irurtzun, a difficult 5.0 miles (8.1 km) path. This route ascends Monte Erga, featuring oak and impressive beech forests. Local runners also love the Erga – Ahizpa Txikia loop from Irurtzun, a 6.9 miles (11.1 km) trail leading through mountainous terrain and dense forests, often completed in about 1 hour 45 minutes. Jogging around Irurtzun is defined by abrupt and mountainous terrain, the Larraun River, and extensive oak and beech forests. The network offers options for easy, moderate, and difficult running routes. The routes in Irurtzun are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 5.0 stars from 3 reviews. More than 200 runners have used komoot to explore Irurtzun's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many running routes are available around Irurtzun?
A: Irurtzun offers a diverse selection of over 20 running routes. These trails cater to various fitness levels, with options ranging from easy paths to more challenging, difficult ascents through the mountainous terrain.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on jogging trails in Irurtzun?
A: The jogging trails around Irurtzun are characterized by &lt;strong&gt;abrupt and mountainous terrain&lt;/strong&gt;, featuring varied elevations. You'll encounter steep sections, extensive oak and beech forests, and picturesque areas along the Larraun River, including the stunning "Dos Hermanas" Gorge.

Q: Are there any easy running routes for beginners in Irurtzun?
A: Yes, while many routes are moderate to difficult, there is at least one easy jogging route available. The region's diverse landscape ensures options for all skill levels, allowing beginners to find suitable paths to enjoy the scenery.

Q: What are some notable natural landmarks or viewpoints I can see while running in Irurtzun?
A: Joggers can enjoy several impressive natural features. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e971506390/vulture-s-balcony-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Vulture's Balcony Trail&lt;/a&gt; leads to the Mirador Balcón de los Buitres, offering panoramic views of the Sakana corridor and the Irurtzun valley. You might also encounter the dramatic "Dos Hermanas" Gorge carved by the Larraun River, and the scenic Monte Erga, crowned by the Ermita de la Trinidad.

Q: Are there any long-distance running options or multi-use trails in the area?
A: Absolutely. A significant highlight for runners is the &lt;strong&gt;Plazaola Greenway&lt;/strong&gt;, an old railway line converted into a natural path. This Greenway spans almost 60 km, offering extensive sections ideal for long-distance running. While some parts are unpaved, providing a natural surface, be aware that some tunnels might require a light.

Q: What do other runners say about the trails in Irurtzun?
A: The running routes in Irurtzun are highly rated by the komoot community, with an impressive average score of 5.0 stars from 3 reviews. Runners often praise the varied terrain, the challenging ascents, and the beautiful natural landscapes, particularly the dense forests and panoramic viewpoints.

Q: Can I find routes that take me through forests or offer shade?
A: Yes, many routes in Irurtzun traverse dense woodlands. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38162034" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Erga loop from Irurtzun&lt;/a&gt; ascends Monte Erga, featuring impressive oak and beech forests that provide ample shade and a refreshing running environment.

Q: Are there any circular running routes available?
A: Yes, Irurtzun offers several circular running routes. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38160290" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Erga – Ahizpa Txikia loop from Irurtzun&lt;/a&gt; is a popular 11.1 km trail that allows you to start and finish in the same location, leading through mountainous terrain and forests.

Q: What kind of wildlife or natural beauty can I expect to see on the trails?
A: The region's transitional climate between Mediterranean and Atlantic Navarra results in a diverse range of flora, including various types of oaks and beeches. Along the "Dos Hermanas" Gorge, you might spot raptors, and the riverside vegetation features willows and alders, adding to the scenic beauty of your run.

Q: Are there any historical or cultural points of interest near the running paths?
A: While primarily focused on natural beauty, you can find some historical elements. The Monte Erga is home to the Hermitage of the Holy Trinity (Ermita de la Trinidad). Additionally, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3552575" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Aixita Castle Ruins&lt;/a&gt; are a nearby attraction that can be explored, offering a glimpse into the region's past.

Q: What are some of the highest points or summits I can reach on a run?
A: For those seeking challenging ascents, routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38162034" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Erga loop from Irurtzun&lt;/a&gt; will take you up Monte Erga, which stands at 1,088 meters. Other nearby summits include &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3149161" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Eltxumendi Summit&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2839727" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Txurregi Summit (1,121 m)&lt;/a&gt;, offering rewarding views after a strenuous climb.

Q: Are there any routes that offer views of the Larraun River?
A: Yes, the Larraun River is a prominent natural feature in the area. The "Dos Hermanas" Gorge, carved by the Larraun River, is a spectacular sight. The path leading to the Mirador Balcón de los Buitres also runs through a beautiful natural landscape shaped by the erosion of the Larraun River, providing picturesque riverside views.
tips: It owes its name to the fact that from this attractive panoramic point it is possible to see vultures. The arrival path runs through a splendid natural landscape created by the erosion of the Larraun river. From the viewpoint you will have some beautiful postcards towards the Sakana corridor and the Irurtzun valley to the city of Pamplona/Iruña.

faqs.question: What is the estimated running time for this circular route?
faqs.answer: The estimated duration for completing this 5 km route is about 46 minutes for running. If you prefer to hike, it would take approximately 2 hours.

faqs.question: Is The Vulture's Balcony – running circular from Irurtzun suitable for beginners?
faqs.answer: This route is classified as moderate difficulty, requiring good fitness for running due to its elevation gain of around 258 meters. While generally accessible, some sections, especially rocky areas or in case of snow, might require sure-footedness. There is an easier, shorter detour available, the SL®-NA 138.1 Artadia, which avoids the higher ascent if you're looking for a less challenging option.

faqs.question: Where can I park to access the trailhead for this route?
faqs.answer: The trail starts and ends in Irurtzun, making it a convenient circular route. While specific parking details aren't provided, Irurtzun is the main access point for the trailhead.

faqs.question: What kind of terrain can I expect on this running route?
faqs.answer: You'll encounter a diverse mix of surfaces, including mostly paved sections, dedicated hiking paths, and accessible forest paths. The route ascends through oak forests and pastures, with some rocky areas, particularly around the 'Dos Hermanas' peaks. A portion of the trail also passes through a tunnel of the old Plazaola train line.

faqs.question: What wildlife might I spot on the trail?
faqs.answer: As its name suggests, the 'Balcón de los Buitres' (Vulture's Balcony) is a prime location to frequently see griffon vultures soaring overhead. Information panels along the route provide details about the area's wildlife, particularly these impressive birds.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to run this trail?
faqs.answer: The trail can be enjoyed throughout the year, but conditions vary. Some sections might be risky in case of snow. For the most pleasant experience, consider running during spring or autumn when temperatures are milder and the natural scenery is vibrant.

faqs.question: What are the main scenic highlights along the route?
faqs.answer: The absolute highlight is the Balcón de los Buitres Viewpoint, offering spectacular panoramic views of the 'Dos Hermanas' rock formations, the Larraun River, the Sakana corridor, and the Irurtzun valley extending towards Pamplona/Iruña. You'll also pass through beautiful oak groves and pastures, and a tunnel of the old Plazaola train line.

faqs.question: Does this route connect with other trails?
faqs.answer: Yes, this route follows 100% of the 'Saien balkoiaren ibilbidea' and also incorporates sections of the 'Plazaolako bide berdea' (41%), 'Ruta ciclable Agurain-Irurtzun Ziklobidea' (18%), and the 'Asturian Way' (11%), among others.

faqs.question: Is this running route dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: The trail is generally suitable for dogs, but given the presence of wildlife like griffon vultures and the varied terrain, it's always recommended to keep your dog on a leash to ensure their safety and respect the natural environment.

faqs.question: Are there any permits or fees required to use this trail?
faqs.answer: No, there are no specific permits or entrance fees required to access and enjoy this running route.

faqs.question: What should I wear or bring for a run on this trail?
faqs.answer: Given the moderate difficulty and varied terrain, wear appropriate running shoes with good grip. Layers of clothing are advisable, especially for changing weather conditions. While not explicitly mentioned, carrying water is always recommended for any physical activity. Consider bringing binoculars for birdwatching, especially for the griffon vultures.
